answer:
\boxed{D}

A double diminished interval is a tritone (augmented fourth/diminished fifth), which spans 6 semitones. From **♯F (G♭)**, the possible double diminished intervals downward are **♯F–♭B (6 semitones)** and **♯F–E♭ (6 semitones)**, totaling **2**. No other intervals within an octave fit this definition.
